summary: Walt Disney discusses his decision to diversify beyond animated cartoons into nature and live-action films to utilize his studio's growing talent and break monotony. He also addresses the competitive threat of television, arguing that quality films will still draw audiences out of their homes, and emphasizes that his films are primarily entertainment, even if they contain educational value.

principles.statement: Diversify your projects to make use of the talent you've developed and to keep the work fun.
principles.rationale: Developing a surplus of talent in one area (like animation) creates an opportunity to expand into new mediums (like nature or live-action films), which also helps break the monotony of doing the same thing.
principles.source_quote: I developed a lot of talent over the years and I couldn't use all of this talent for the cartoon so I went into the nature films and then uh just for the fun of it we went in doing films with humans

principles.rationale: While new mediums like television make it easy for people to stay home, creating the right kind of compelling entertainment will always motivate audiences to go out.
principles.source_quote: television uh can be competition yes in other words it's a it's a medium that can keep people in their homes instead of get them out but again if you have the right kind of films the people go out

principles.statement: Never label your work as educational; it must be entertainment first.
principles.rationale: Audiences are primarily looking to be entertained. You can still provide educational value, but it should be a byproduct of the entertainment rather than the label.
principles.source_quote: I'm not labeling anything as educational it's all entertainment but I mean people can uh be educated and still be entertained

heuristics: Don't label it educational; call it entertainment.
If you have the right kind of films, the people go out.

signature_quotes.text: I'm not labeling anything as educational it's all entertainment but I mean people can uh be educated and still be entertained
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the educational value of his nature films.

signature_quotes.text: if you have the right kind of films the people go out
signature_quotes.context: Addressing television as competition to cinema.

signature_quotes.text: I developed a lot of talent over the years and I couldn't use all of this talent for the cartoon so I went into the nature films
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why he diversified beyond animation.

anti_patterns.description: Labeling films or stories explicitly as 'educational'.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Audiences seek entertainment first. Labeling content as educational can deter them, whereas focusing on entertainment allows you to educate them naturally as a byproduct.
